category_title: Trouble-makers who resist through sleeping, mobile phone use, and makeup
scenario_title: Transition to textbook reading and disciplinary defiance
minimal_context: The student is sitting at the back of the classroom during a Korean history lesson as the class transitions from watching a video-clip to reading the textbook.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Now that we have watched the video-clip, let's start going through the textbook.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sitting at the back of the classroom without a textbook on the desk, continuing to talk) Oh, teacher, please forgive me this once!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You are still talking instead of looking at the textbook. I am giving you a minus point.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Slumps over the desk) Right! I’m going to sleep!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I am insisting on the penalty point. Please open your book.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stands up again, saying it in fun but not stopping talking) Oh, I won’t study, I will sue the teacher for giving me a penalty point!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Now that we have watched the video-clip, let's start going through the textbook.

STUDENT: (Sitting at the back of the classroom without a textbook on the desk, continuing to talk) Oh, teacher, please forgive me this once!

TUTOR: You are still talking instead of looking at the textbook. I am giving you a minus point.

STUDENT: (Slumps over the desk) Right! I’m going to sleep!

TUTOR: I am insisting on the penalty point. Please open your book.

STUDENT: (Stands up again, saying it in fun but not stopping talking) Oh, I won’t study, I will sue the teacher for giving me a penalty point!
